thm:1.2.1.summary: Imports BMS1 thm:1.10: for $K$ algebraically closed, $p$-adic, and smooth proper $X/\mathcal{O}_K$, there is a perfect $A_{\mathrm{inf}}$-complex of cohomology recovering crystalline / Hodge-Tate / de Rham / étale. Setup statement; the rest of the paper extends to absolute setting.

thm:3.4.13.summary: The morphism $\eta: \mathrm{Spf}(\mathbb{Z}_p) \to \mathrm{WCart}^{\mathrm{HT}}$ extends to an isomorphism of stacks. Identifies the Hodge-Tate locus of the Cartier-Witt stack with $\mathrm{Spf}(\mathbb{Z}_p)$ — the geometric basis for Sen's theorem and the entire absolute prismatic story.

thm:3.5.8.summary: $\mathcal{D}(\mathrm{WCart}^{\mathrm{HT}}) \to \mathcal{D}(\mathbb{Z}[\Theta])$, $\mathscr{E} \mapsto (\mathscr{E}_\eta, \Theta_\mathscr{E})$ is fully faithful. Lets quasi-coherent complexes on the Hodge-Tate stack be tracked through fibre + Sen-operator data.

thm:3.6.7.summary: Frobenius pushout square: for $\mathscr{E}$ quasi-coherent on $\mathrm{WCart}^{\mathrm{HT}}$, the diagram of base changes along Frobenius determines a pullback square. The structural input that makes prismatic cohomology computable via the Cartier-Witt stack.

thm:3.8.3.summary: For odd $p$, $\mathcal{D}(\mathrm{WCart}) \to \mathcal{D}([\mathrm{Spf}(\mathbb{Z}_p)])$-style functor via $q$-de Rham cohomology is fully faithful. Categorical incarnation of the $q$-de Rham comparison.

thm:3.9.1.summary: Sen's theorem (imported): for $V$ finite-dimensional over $C$ with continuous semilinear $\mathrm{Gal}(K)$-action, there exists a unique $V_\infty \subseteq V$ + endomorphism $\Theta$ describing the Galois action via differentiation. The classical analytic-Galois result that lifts to the Cartier-Witt picture.

thm:4.3.6.summary: Discreteness criterion: for $p$-adic formal $\mathfrak{X}/\overline{A}$ satisfying a local condition $(\ast)$, prismatic-side data is concentrated in degree zero. The technical input that makes integral computations behave.

thm:4.6.1.summary: Char-$p$ absolute prismatic = crystalline: for $X$ quasisyntomic $\mathbb{F}_p$-scheme, $R\Gamma_{\mathbbl{\Delta}}(X) \simeq R\Gamma_{\mathrm{crys}}(X / \mathbb{Z}_p)$. The absolute version of Bhatt–Scholze's relative crystalline comparison; the headline equivalence of the paper.

thm:5.4.1.summary: Berthelot (imported): formal scheme $\mathfrak{X}/\mathrm{Spf}(\mathbb{Z}_p)$ smooth has crystalline cohomology recovering de Rham of the special fibre. The classical input the absolute prismatic comparison generalizes.

thm:5.4.2.summary: For animated commutative $R$ with $\overline{R} = \mathbb{F}_p \otimes^L R$: $\mathbbl{\Delta}_{\overline{R}} \simeq \widehat{\mathrm{dR}}_R$ canonically. Identifies absolute prismatic cohomology mod $p$ with completed derived de Rham — the local computation underlying thm:4.6.1.

thm:6.2.1.summary: BMS2 imported: for $R$ quasiregular semiperfectoid, $\pi_* \mathrm{THH}(R)^\wedge_p$ concentrated in even degrees, with $\pi_* \mathrm{TC}^-(R)^\wedge_p$ recovering $A_{\mathrm{inf}}(R)$ structure. Topological-Hochschild input.

thm:6.2.4.summary: BMS2 motivic filtration (imported): $\mathrm{Fil}^\bullet_T \mathrm{TP}(R)^\wedge_p$ realized via syntomic-site colimit. Connects topological-Hochschild filtrations to the prismatic side.

thm:7.1.1.summary: Exact sequence for quasiregular semiperfect $\mathbb{F}_p$-algebras: kernel of $R^\flat \twoheadrightarrow R$ controls obstruction classes. Used as input for the syntomic Chern-class computation.

thm:7.3.5.summary: Crystalline first Chern class: $\widehat{c}_1^{\mathrm{crys}}: R\Gamma_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(\mathrm{Spec}(R), \mathbf{G}_m)^\wedge[-1] \to R\Gamma_{\mathrm{crys}}$ fits into a fiber sequence. The cohomological avatar of Chern-class theory in the prismatic setting.

thm:7.5.6.summary: Synthetic first Chern class: $\widehat{c}_1^{\mathrm{syn}}: R\Gamma_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(\mathrm{Spec}(R), \mathbf{G}_m)^\wedge[-1] \to R\Gamma_{\mathrm{syn}}$. Lifts the crystalline Chern class to the syntomic level — bridge between étale and prismatic via the syntomic comparison.

thm:7.6.2.summary: De Rham Chern class: $c_1^{\mathrm{dR}}: R\Gamma(\mathrm{Spec}(R)_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}, \mathbf{G}_m)[-1] \to \mathrm{Fil}^1_{\mathrm{Hod}} \widehat{\mathrm{dR}}_R$. The de Rham filtered version, completing the Chern-class trifecta with crystalline + syntomic.

thm:8.1.9.summary: Étale-syntomic comparison: for $p$-complete animated $R$, the comparison map $\alpha_R: R\Gamma_{\mathrm{\acute{e}t}}(\mathrm{Spec}(R), \mathbb{Z}_p) \to R\Gamma_{\mathrm{syn}}(R, \mathbb{Z}_p(\bullet))$ has explicit fibre. The integration of étale cohomology into the prismatic / syntomic framework.

thm:8.3.1.summary: Combined Chern-class isomorphism: for $p$-complete animated $R$, the direct sum of synthetic Chern classes is canonical — assembling crystalline + syntomic + de Rham Chern classes into one universal object. Headline structural-coherence statement.

edges.note: BMS1 setup uses BMS2's QRS-perfectoid structure as input.
Fibre-and-Sen-operator description requires identification of HT locus with $\mathrm{Spf}(\mathbb{Z}_p)$.
Frobenius pushout uses the HT-stack quasi-coherent description.
$q$-de Rham fully-faithfulness uses Frobenius pushout square.
Sen's theorem corresponds geometrically to the HT-stack identification.
Discreteness criterion uses Frobenius pushout to control degeneration.
Char-$p$ comparison reduces via animated derived de Rham.
Recovers Berthelot's classical statement as a special case.
Discreteness criterion underlies the canonical isomorphism.
QRS-exact-sequence input runs through BMS2 motivic filtration.
Crystalline Chern class uses the QRS-perfect kernel sequence.
QRS-perfectoid base structure underlies the fiber sequence.
Synthetic Chern class lifts crystalline Chern class to syntomic level.
De Rham Chern class is the de-Rham specialization of synthetic.
Étale-syntomic comparison runs through the Chern-class machinery.
Combined Chern-class isomorphism uses étale-syntomic comparison.
Combined Chern class assembles crystalline, syntomic, and de Rham forms.
Introduction theorem provides the relative-prismatic base case the absolute version (thm:8.3.1) extends.

main_results.description: Sections 1, 4.6, 8. Char-$p$ absolute prismatic = crystalline (thm:4.6.1); étale-syntomic comparison (thm:8.1.9); combined Chern-class isomorphism (thm:8.3.1).

Cartier_Witt_stack.description: Section 3. The Cartier-Witt stack $\mathrm{WCart}$ + its Hodge-Tate locus $\mathrm{WCart}^{\mathrm{HT}}$ — the geometric host of absolute prismatic cohomology.

char_p_comparison.description: Sections 4.6, 5.4. Absolute char-$p$ prismatic recovers crystalline cohomology, and animated derived de Rham mod $p$.

BMS2_input.description: Section 6. Imported BMS2 results on QRS-perfectoid topological Hochschild homology + motivic filtration.

Chern_class_machinery.description: Section 7. First Chern classes on étale + crystalline + de Rham + syntomic levels, assembled into universal coherent system.

BMS2.title: THH and integral p-adic Hodge theory

Prismatic.title: Prisms and prismatic cohomology
Prismatic.role: Direct predecessor; absolute version of relative prismatic.

BerthelotCrysCoh.title: Cohomologie cristalline des schémas de caractéristique $p>0$

Sen.title: Continuous cohomology and p-adic Galois representations
